matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
seeded_markers: rs1050828 (G6PD A- marker) (G6PD A- deficiency marker)
G6PD deficiency class model (G6PD enzyme-deficiency model)
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a G6PD red-cell oxidative metabolism thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to NADPH production, red-cell oxidative stress defense, glutathione recycling, and hemolysis susceptibility and G6PD deficiency review context, Drug/food-triggered hemolysis susceptibility. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support.

Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the NADPH production, red-cell oxidative stress defense, glutathione recycling, and hemolysis susceptibility pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched rs1050828 (G6PD A- marker) at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the G6PD prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a G6PD red-cell oxidative metabolism thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to NADPH production, red-cell oxidative stress defense, glutathione recycling, and hemolysis susceptibility and G6PD deficiency review context, Drug/food-triggered hemolysis susceptibility. The local G6PD bundle is red-cell oxidative metabolism oriented. Interpret markers through exact allele class, zygosity, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context rather than as stand-alone deterministic calls. G6PD variants can alter or tag NADPH production, red-cell oxidative stress defense, glutathione recycling, and hemolysis susceptibility; effect direction depends on allele class, tissue, dosage, and biological context.
